assertions.expectation: Explains the difference between Rapid and Stable channels — including that Rapid has no SLA for upgrade stability while Stable carries a full SLA, and clearly distinguishes minor version vs patch version differences between the channels
assertions.reason: Response clearly explains that Rapid has no upgrade stability SLA while Stable has full GKE SLA coverage, and describes timing differences between channels (2 weeks vs 6-8 weeks). It distinguishes between version types in the maintenance exclusion section.

assertions.expectation: Notes that channel changes don't trigger an immediate upgrade or downgrade
assertions.reason: Explicitly states 'No immediate upgrade — your cluster won't downgrade or change versions during migration' and 'Current 1.32 version: Will remain until Stable channel catches up'

assertions.expectation: Warns that if 1.30 isn't yet available on Stable, the cluster may not receive patches until Stable catches up
assertions.reason: The response mentions that the cluster will remain on current version until Stable catches up, but doesn't specifically warn about potential patch delivery delays for version 1.32 specifically, only mentions slower security patches in general

assertions.expectation: Provides the gcloud command to change the release channel
assertions.reason: Provides the exact command: 'gcloud container clusters update CLUSTER_NAME --region REGION --release-channel stable'

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking version availability on Stable before switching (gcloud container get-server-config)
assertions.reason: Includes the specific command in pre-migration checks: 'gcloud container get-server-config --region REGION --format="yaml(channels.STABLE.validVersions[])"'

assertions.expectation: Discusses the impact on auto-upgrade timing going forward
assertions.reason: Extensively discusses auto-upgrade behavior changes, including timeline expectations and how upgrades will follow Stable channel timeline going forward

assertions.expectation: Mentions that the Extended channel is an option if they need even slower cadence (with cost implications)
assertions.reason: The Extended channel is not mentioned anywhere in the response. Only discusses Rapid, Regular, and Stable channels

assertions.expectation: Advises coordinating the change during a maintenance window
assertions.reason: While the response mentions setting up maintenance windows for future upgrades, it doesn't specifically advise coordinating the actual channel change during a maintenance window - it states the migration has zero downtime
